HomeSeoTitle: Thrive In Chaos – Belgian Alternative Rock Band
HomeSeoDescription: Thrive In Chaos is a Belgian alternative rock band from Melsele, blending post-rock, stoner and progressive influences. Listen on Spotify and explore our music and live performances.
LineUpStijnBio: With a deep, commanding voice and an emotion-first delivery, Stijn brings every line to life, whether it's a quiet confession or a full-throttle roar. As the band's lyricist, he shapes the stories at the heart of the music, and on stage he turns them into something you feel, not just hear. Intense, magnetic, and impossible to ignore.
LineUpMaximBio: Maxim is the engine behind the band: high-energy, razor-tight drumming that drives every song forward without ever losing control. Off the kit, he's also the visual mind of the band, creating most of the artwork and shaping the look that comes with the sound. And every now and then, he steps into the writing too, adding lyrics that hit just as hard as his rhythms.
LineUpEmielBio: The youngest in the band, Emiel brings a raw edge that keeps everything loud, gritty, and alive. His rhythm work is the spark that turns riffs into full-on chaos, and his energy on stage is impossible to miss. Offstage, he's the one keeping the band connected and active online, running most of the social media. Loud, fearless, and very likely the crowd favorite.
LineUpArnoBio: Arno, also known as Mister Brar Brar, is easily the loudest force in the band. His bass lines hit hard, stay tight, and keep everything grounded, shaking the foundations as the chaos rages above. And if you're a fan of bass solos… don't worry, he'll deliver.
LineUpJonasBio: The newest addition to the lineup, Jonas has quickly become a key part of the band's creative core. A multi-instrumentalist and major contributor to the songwriting, he helps shape the sound both on and off the stage, handling music production duties and constantly pushing new ideas forward.
MusicIntro: Thrive In Chaos is a Belgian five-piece rock band that blends atmospheric soundscapes with dynamic guitar work, creating music that shifts between calm, immersive layers and heavier, high-energy moments. Their songs draw from a wide range of rock influences while embracing the open, expansive feel often found in post-rock. Fueled by passion and a relentless drive to evolve, Thrive In Chaos is gearing up to push further, play louder, and take on even bigger stages.
TechBacklineIntro: The band will provide their own backline, which includes the following equipment:
TechPAIntro: The PA, provided by the organizer, needs to be of sufficient quality/power for the venue. We are however flexible and can provide some of our own gear (as outlined below) in case the venue does not have access to certain materials, if communicated in advance. In an ideal situation, the venue PA system includes:
TechPAUSB: USB input to play intro music
TechPAEngineer: A sound engineer who can set up, connect, dismantle, and operate the complete audio installation
TechPAMiking: Our preferred method of connecting to the PA is through miking the instruments/amplifiers. The bass amplifier and one of the guitar amplifiers (Lead) have speaker-simulated DI outputs available if there are not enough microphones present. If needed, the band can provide the following equipment, if communicated in advance:
TechMonitorIntro: The required monitor setup depends on the size of the venue/stage. While not ideal, for smaller venues, it is possible to play without a PA system and with a single monitor for our vocalist. Please refer to the below table to see the required monitor equipment per stage size:
TechStageplanIntro: We are flexible in terms of stage setup depending on the venue. If possible, please provide mounting options for our banner (300cm x 200cm). We require a minimum of 6 free power outlets (Type E / Type F) on stage for the band to use. Ideally 3 at the rear of the stage, and 3 at the front of the stage (mainly near the guitar and bass players). In general, we try to achieve the following ideal stage plan:
